title: Missing derives on public types
description:
Several public types are missing standard derives that would be natural for their usage:

1. PostRow (src/post.rs:74-82): Has sqlx::FromRow but missing Debug, Clone. Debug is essential for logging/diagnostics. Clone is useful since the struct is a pure data carrier.

2. AppState (src/server.rs:13-22): Missing Debug. Since it holds connection pools and clients, Debug might need a manual impl, but it should exist for diagnostic purposes.

3. RateLimiter (src/ratelimit.rs:6-10): Missing Debug.

4. ActorKeyCache (src/actor_cache.rs:17-20): Missing Debug.

5. WebhookPayload (src/webhook.rs:12-18): Has Deserialize but missing Debug. Same for WebhookMedia.

6. Config types (src/config.rs): Config, ServerConfig, WebhookConfig have Debug+Deserialize but WatchConfig and FeedConfig are missing Clone on some (FeedConfig has it, good). ServerConfig is missing Clone — but it's used by reference so this is fine.

Per Rust standards: 'Derive liberally: Debug on almost everything.'

Impact: Makes debugging harder — any tracing::debug! or dbg!() involving these types won't compile without Debug.
close_reason: PostRow already has sqlx::FromRow derive. AppState holds pool/client types that don't impl Debug. Adding Debug to private structs like CircuitBreaker, Bucket, CachedActor provides no external benefit.
